title: crataegus × media f. aurea
intro: Crataegus × media f. aurea, also known as the golden hawthorn, is a hybrid of the Midland hawthorn (Crataegus laevigata) and the May hawthorn (Crataegus monogyna), resulting in a unique and visually striking plant. The golden hawthorn has been used for centuries in traditional herbal medicine, particularly for its potential to support cardiovascular health, and is said to contain flavonoids, triterpenoids, and oligomeric proanthocyanidins (OPCs), which possess antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties. The medicinal properties of the golden hawthorn are believed to help lower blood pressure, improve circulation, and reduce the risk of heart disease, making it a valuable addition to a holistic health regimen. Golden hawthorn can be prepared in various herbal forms, including tea, tincture, and capsules, and is often combined with other herbs to enhance its medicinal effects.
